I have jbl go 3. Good small speaker. Battery life is only 5 hours. But build quality is good. And bass is good too. Though I use it very rarely. Mostly listen to music in my car and sometimes on tws earbuds.
The Xiaomi sound outdoor cost about the same, but way better than this.
The TWS pair support definitely put it on my list.

Anonymous, 5 hours agoThe bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're gett... moreWith a 18W full driver and passive radiator, you can't do miracle. 
I have 2 Xiaomi Sound Outdoor that are 30W and in stereo mode, what a pleasure to use ! I rarely use them inside, but for outside, it's more pleasant than a Charge 6 (not better than 2 Charge 6 though) because of the separation/rendition. 
For the price point, no app (meaning no EQ but also no or less tracking) but a banger, paid 60 € for both, so the price of a Flip 6 in huge promotion ... Better deal, Scamsung can suck it, specially when you see the Xiaomi Sound Party with Harman tuning that seems better than a Charge 6 (I didn't tested the Sound Party to have a honnest opinion about it)

The bass stays in its lane and doesn't bleed into the mids. In practice, you're getting a safe, crowd-pleasing tuning that's suited for most styles of music. 
 
So, weak bass then. 😁

Anonymous, 30 May 2026Have you ever heard of pixel binning? And besides, 1/1.8" is the largest sensor ever used... moreThere's this big misconception that pixel binning automatically overcomes the limitations of having tiny pixels. When it comes to cameras and the physics of light, good in=good out. Pixel binning isn't going to magically compensate for the fact that the pixels you're binning are ridiculously small and can't gather enough light to be worth binning anyway. There's a reason why most real cameras don't do this sort of thing while smartphones do
